Guide
Loading...
PH Wire Colors (PEC 2017)
Red — Line (Hot)Main live supply from MCB
Blue — NeutralReturn path, always connected
Brown/Amber — TravelerLinks switches together (2-way, 3-way)
Red — Switched LiveControlled output to fixture
Wire Tray
Tap a wire, then tap its two terminal points on the scene
COMPONENT INFO
Terminals
How it works
TAP HERE
Switch Wiring Simulator
Choose a circuit to wire up — start with 1-Switch if you're new
1-SWITCH
Single Location Control
One switch controls one light from one place. The simplest circuit — a single wire break in the Line.
You'll learn: Line wire, neutral wire, switch terminals (L, LOAD), and why neutral never goes through the switch.
2-SWITCH
Two Location Control
Two switches share control of one light — flip either one to toggle. Used on staircases.
You'll learn: 3-way switch, traveler wires, COM terminal, and why two switches need 3 wires between them.
3-SWITCH
Three Location Control
Three switches, one light — any switch toggles it. The center one is an INTERMEDIATE (4-way) switch.
You'll learn: 4-way intermediate switch, L1/L2/L3/L4 terminals, and how XOR logic controls the light state.
WIRED!
